comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Plan-review gate — manual run (epic 5fd2 converged 3-pass process)

**GATE: PASS** (no `block` decisions). DET floor: check-ac pass, clarity pass.
Rubric: 31 v8 criteria (all overlays). Pass-1 Opus (29 findings) -> Pass-2 aggregate/agentic Sonnet -> Pass-3 decisions: {'advisory': 10, 'dropped': 7, 'indeterminate': 12}.

Run via docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/run_review_configepic.py
(adapted from run_review_foundation.py). Raw: configepic_review_3pass.json.

### High-confidence advisories (9) — folded into the plan/children:
- Docs AC7 (README storage + architecture.md) uncovered by children -> assigned to child 14b8.
- Orphan/no-auto-migrate behavior mentioned in body but not pinned by an AC -> AC added to this epic.
- `tracker.dir` shape under-specified; branch-validator rejection cases untested -> AC added to 14b8/5436.
- Custom-branch never verified end-to-end (only create/list/show) -> 5436 e2e broadened to a write+sync path.
- G6: the two `tracker_dir()` impls differ — reads.py does a git work-tree validation gate; unify must preserve it -> AC added to 8f43.

Note: the conf=0.71 advisory claiming `_advisory_lock.py:498,533` doesn't exist is a **false positive** — verified the file + literals exist at those exact lines; the plan citation is correct (correctly ranked advisory, not block).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Epic<->child reconciliation (post plan-review)

Propagated the plan-review refinements both ways so the epic and its 5 children agree:

**Epic updated to match child refinements:**
- AC4 broadened: init round-trip must exercise a write+sync path (not just create/list/show) — owned by 5436.
- New AC: `dir`-shape validation rejects unsafe paths — owned by 14b8.
- Unify-duplicate AC + Constraints note: reads.py tracker_dir() git work-tree validation gate must be preserved on unification — owned by 8f43.
- Constraints: fsck mismatch mechanism pinned (precedence-resolved 'configured' vs worktree/symlink 'mounted') — owned by 5436.
- Suggested-tasks #1/#5 reworded to the broadened scope.

**Child updated to own a new epic AC:**
- 14b8 now owns documenting the orphan/no-auto-migrate behavior (epic AC) + README/architecture.md docs, and owns the branch+dir validator UNIT tests.

**Overlap resolved:** validator accept/reject UNIT tests -> 14b8 (config-level); 5436 keeps init/e2e/regression/fsck and explicitly defers the unit tests to 14b8.

All 6 tickets re-pass the DET floor (check-ac + clarity) after the edits.

compiled_state.description:
## Context
Two store identifiers are currently fixed: the worktree/symlink dir `.tickets-tracker` (env-overridable via `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` but NOT via project TOML) and the orphan **branch name** `tickets` (hardcoded in ~20 git invocations, no override at all). Make both overridable through project config, defaulting to the current values so every existing repo is unaffected.

## Proposed design
Add a typed `tracker` config section in `src/rebar/config.py` so the auto-derived env names line up with the established ones:
- `tracker.dir = ".tickets-tracker"`  -> auto-env `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` (already the canonical override; keep `TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR` as legacy alias).
- `tracker.branch = "tickets"`        -> auto-env `REBAR_TRACKER_BRANCH`.
Register in `_SECTION_CLASSES`, `_SECTIONS` (coercers: dir = relative-safe path; branch = valid git ref via a small validator), and `TrackerConfig` dataclass on `Config`.

Introduce a single resolver `tickets_branch(root=None)` next to `tracker_dir()`, and ROUTE BOTH through the resolved typed Config (full precedence: `-c` flag > env > project > user > default), not env-only. `tracker_dir()` currently consults env only (config.py:81-87) — it must read resolved config like the branch resolver.

## Call sites to migrate off the literals
- Branch `tickets` / `origin/tickets` (replace with `tickets_branch()` + f"origin/{branch}"):
  - `_commands/init.py` (lines ~272-295, 281): orphan/worktree creation, fetch, mount.
  - `_store/sync.py` (read reconverge, ~83-153) and `_store/push.py` (push refspec line 112, merge ~124-165).
  - `_engine/rebar_reconciler/_concurrency.py:56`, `_advisory_lock.py:498,533`.
  - `_engine_support/reads.py:187` (rev-parse verify).
  - `_commands/fsck.py:227-236` (PUSH_PENDING vs origin/<branch>).
- Worktree dir `.tickets-tracker` (replace with `tracker_dir()` / its basename):
  - The DUPLICATE `tracker_dir()` in `_engine_support/reads.py:99-149` must be unified with / kept in sync with `config.tracker_dir()`. NOTE: that copy is NOT a pure resolver — it also runs a git work-tree validation gate (`rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree`); preserve that on unification (keep it, or split path-resolution from validation explicitly), don't silently drop it.
  - `_commands/init.py:152,199,418,461-469`: dir creation, the git `_exclude` it writes, the symlink name, the worktree-path resolver. The exclude must use the resolved basename so a custom dir is auto-gitignored.

## Constraints / decisions
- Defaults unchanged; pure additive. Existing repos and `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` keep working.
- Project config (committed `[tool.rebar]` in pyproject.toml / rebar.toml) is the right layer for branch name so all clones of one repo agree; the discovery walk stops at `.git` so it resolves at the repo root before the worktree exists.
- OUT OF SCOPE: renaming an EXISTING store (that is a migration). Both values are read at `init` time and thereafter; document that changing them on an initialized repo orphans the old branch/dir and is not auto-migrated. fsck SHOULD warn if a configured branch/dir does not match what is mounted — comparing the precedence-resolved config ("configured") against the actual checked-out branch + worktree/symlink target ("mounted", via `git worktree list` / the symlink).
- Validate branch against `git check-ref-format`-style rules (reject spaces, `..`, leading `-`, `~^:?*[`, trailing `/` or `.lock`). Validate `dir` shape too: a safe relative path (no absolute / `..` traversal) unless supplied via `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tracker.dir` and `tracker.branch` exist as typed config keys with the precedence chain and appear in `rebar config` output + docs/config.md.
- [ ] A single `tracker_dir()` and a new `tickets_branch()` resolver are the only sources of these values; the duplicate resolver is removed/unified **with its git work-tree validation gate preserved** (see Constraints).
- [ ] All git invocations listed above use the resolvers (no remaining `"tickets"` / `.tickets-tracker` literals in code paths).
- [ ] `rebar init` honors both on a fresh repo: custom branch + custom dir create + mount + gitignore correctly; round-trips through a **write+sync** path (create -> push/pull or reconcile dry-run -> read-back), not just create/list/show.
- [ ] Defaults reproduce todays behavior byte-for-byte (regression tests green).
- [ ] Branch-name validation rejects invalid refs with a clear ConfigError.
- [ ] `dir`-shape validation rejects unsafe paths (absolute / `..` traversal) with a clear ConfigError.
- [ ] Docs updated: README storage section + docs/config.md + docs/architecture.md note configurability.

- [ ] The orphan / no-auto-migrate behavior (changing dir/branch on an initialized repo) is explicitly DOCUMENTED (plan body mentioned it; now an AC pins it).

## Suggested child tasks
1. config: add TrackerConfig section + coercers + branch-ref AND dir-shape validators + docs (config.md, README storage section, architecture.md; incl. the orphan/no-auto-migrate note).
2. resolvers: add tickets_branch(); route tracker_dir() through resolved config; unify the _engine_support duplicate.
3. migrate branch call sites (init/sync/push/reconciler/fsck/reads).
4. migrate worktree-dir call sites (init exclude/symlink/path resolver).
5. tests: custom dir+branch init e2e exercising a write+sync round-trip; validator accept/reject; regression test defaults; fsck mismatch warning (runnable under `pytest -m "not integration"`).
compiled_state.title: Make the tracker worktree dir + tickets branch name configurable (project config)
